title: tropaeolum peregrinum var. weberbaueri
intro: Tropaeolum peregrinum var. weberbaueri is a variety of the 'Mission Bells' or 'Peregrine' flower, a type of flowering plant in the nasturtium family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Tropaeolum peregrinum var. weberbaueri can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, often in combination with other herbs.
